notes.anchor: as consul, you proved the very man in adorning and amplifying my standing
notes.note: C. Claudius Marcellus, consul 50 BC, had used his consulship to advance Cicero's interests at Rome while Cicero was away governing Cilicia. Cicero writes from the East to thank him.

notes.anchor: running straight into the Etesian winds
notes.note: The Etesians are the steady north-northwesterly winds that blow across the Aegean in high summer; they made the sea-voyage westward from the eastern provinces toward Italy slow and difficult.
